headline: RTP Network Reaches Record Single-Day Transaction Volume of 2.27 Million Payments Valued at $8.62 Billion
summary:
## Historic Milestone Achievement

On May 1, 2026, The Clearing House's Real-Time Payments (RTP) network processed 2.27 million transactions valued at $8.62 billion in a single day, setting new operational records. This represented a significant increase from the previous record of 2.05 million payments set in February 2026, demonstrating accelerating adoption and use-case expansion.

## Monthly Volume Growth Trajectory

Monthly RTP transaction volumes have grown from $28 billion in January 2025 to $195 billion by April 2026—a 595% increase over 15 months. This growth is being driven by increased adoption across tax refunds, digital wallet transfers, earned wage access, and account-to-account transfers. Tax refund disbursements through RTP increased 78% in the first four months of 2026 compared to 2025.

## Market Expansion and Infrastructure Maturity

The RTP network now supports more than 1,100 connected financial institutions with continuous settlement and rich messaging capabilities. Growth is being driven by demand from businesses and individuals seeking faster, more efficient payment settlement alternatives to traditional batch processing.

headline: SWIFT Framework for Cross-Border Retail Payments Goes Live; 25+ Banks Commit to June Launch
summary:
## Global Framework Rollout

SWIFT launched its new framework for cross-border retail payments designed to accelerate settlement and reduce friction in international transactions. More than 25 banks across payment corridors in Australia, Bangladesh, Canada, China, Germany, India, Pakistan, Spain, Thailand, the United Kingdom, and the United States committed to processing payments under the framework by June 2026.

## Framework Standards and Guarantees

Receiving banks are committing to credit customers near-instantly (where local infrastructure allows), ensure no hidden deductions from payments, and provide real-time status updates to sending customers. SWIFT's payments scheme leadership emphasized that the initiative enables international transactions with speed, full-value delivery guarantees, and transparent, fixed fee structures.

## Blockchain Infrastructure Investment

SWIFT announced plans to add a blockchain-based shared ledger to its technological infrastructure in September 2026, designed to enable instant, always-on cross-border transactions at unprecedented scale. Bank of America is preparing to launch a cross-border real-time payments tool in Q3 2026 accessible through SWIFT and its CashPro platform.

headline: FedNow Surpasses 1,500 Participants as Adoption Accelerates Among Community Banks and Credit Unions
summary:
## Participation Growth Milestone

The Federal Reserve's FedNow instant payment service has grown to more than 1,500 participating financial institutions, with approximately 1,400 being community banks and credit unions representing 95% of the service's participants. Major institutions including Capital One and PNC Bank have recently joined the network, broadening FedNow's reach across the banking sector.

## Acceleration in Adoption Rate

FedNow experienced back-to-back quarters of double-digit participation growth from Q1 through Q3 2025. The growth is being driven by consumer and business demand for fast, convenient instant payments. Analysts project total U.S. real-time payment volumes to reach 8 billion transactions in 2026 and 13.9 billion by 2028, representing a compound annual growth rate exceeding 30%.

## Risk Management Enhancements

Federal Reserve Financial Services launched new risk mitigation tools for FedNow participants in April 2026, including a network intelligence API that provides receiver account-level data and historical patterns to enable instant risk insights for participating financial institutions.

headline: Regulators Implement GENIUS Act Framework; FDIC and OCC Issue Stablecoin Issuance Standards
summary:
## Regulatory Implementation Accelerates

U.S. banking regulators accelerated implementation of the GENIUS Act (Genuine Authority for Growth on Useful Services Act), signed into law by President Donald Trump in July 2025. The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation approved a proposed rule establishing Bank Secrecy Act and sanctions compliance standards for FDIC-supervised permitted payment stablecoin issuers, while the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency issued proposed rules for stablecoin activity under the Act.

## GENIUS Act Structure

The Act limits payment stablecoin issuance to three categories: subsidiaries of insured depository institutions, federally qualified non-bank issuers overseen by the OCC, and state-qualified issuers that remain under $10 billion in issuance. The Treasury Department issued its first notice of proposed rulemaking to implement the Act on April 1, 2026.

## Payment Infrastructure Implications

Payment stablecoins offer potential to accelerate settlement, reduce costs, and improve financial inclusion by eliminating multi-day settlement delays. Fintech firms including Stripe, PayPal, and Circle are developing payment use cases for stablecoin technology.

headline: White House Executive Order Directs Financial Regulators to Review Rules Limiting FinTech Access to Payment Rails
summary:
## Regulatory Review Initiative

President Trump signed an executive order directing federal financial regulators to review existing regulations, guidance, supervisory practices, and application processes to encourage financial innovation and competition. The initiative specifically targets rules governing how fintech companies work with banks and access to critical payment infrastructure.

## Emerging Technology Integration

Payment industry leaders are developing agent-based payment capabilities that enable autonomous transactions within AI systems. Visa partnered with OpenAI to allow tokenized, agent-based transactions within large language models, while Mastercard pushed a new framework for machine payments. Coinbase and Robinhood are opening their platforms to AI agents for transactional capabilities.

## Legacy Systems and Modernization Challenge

Legacy payment systems remain anchored to batch-era banking operations, limiting product development velocity. Industry analysts note that the most important strategic shift is the rise of payment orchestration hubs—platforms capable of coordinating multiple payment rails from a single system. FinTech competitors are gaining traction among internationally active small and medium-sized businesses through platforms emphasizing simplicity, transparency, and speed.
